title: stapelia retusa
intro: Stapelia retusa, also known as the carrion flower or ghost flower, is a unique and unusual succulent plant native to South Africa, characterized by its distinctive, strong-smelling blooms resembling rotting flesh to attract pollinators. Traditionally, Stapelia retusa has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its bioactive compounds like alkaloids and glycosides. In herbal preparations, Stapelia retusa is often used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity and side effects.
